name: Friedrich Gulda
abstract: Friedrich Gulda (German: [ˌfʁiːdʁɪç ˈɡʊlda] 16 May 1930 – 27 January 2000) was an Austrian pianist and composer who worked in both the classical and jazz fields.

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: Gulda said, 'I invite you to perform this with me in six months with the Concertgebouw of Amsterdam at the Mozart Festival in Amsterdam.' ... We met in this room ... with Gulda to rehearse the concerto ... And we recorded with the Concertgebouw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Friedrich Gulda wrote a wonderful piece. I wrote my piece called Eurosuite, which was a suite in four or five movements, for that occasion.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Subsequent to that, we did a tour with Friedrich Gulda, a Beethoven expert who had a big band, European big band, all-star band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: I had known him from a time that he visited here in New York... I ran into him in Berlin... we were the judges.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: It was put together by Friedrich Gulda, a wonderful Viennese pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022
